text: Arabic is rich in varieties of linguistic, structural and stylistic features. Among the different branches of Arabic linguistics, Morphology or “Sarf” is the backbone of the process of derivation in Arabic. This research therefore applied library research in order to carry out a detailed morphological analysis of Surah An-Naba’ that falls within the 30th Juz of the Qur’an. This research aimed at amongst others identifying the instances of *fi’il mudhari* (present and future tense verbs), their different patterns *wazan* (morphology) and the pronouns *dhomir* they refer to. It tried to marry the two aspects of Arabic linguistics namely *Nahwu* and *Sarf* in the explanation of the morphology of the Surah in question. The results indicated 16 occurrences of *fi’il mudhari* with different *wazan* and with referring pronouns in different *daf* and *za‘* among others.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
